papers.title_en: NSVQ: Mitigating Codebook Collapse by Stabilizing Encoder Drift in Vector Quantization
papers.authors: Hao Lu, Yongxin Guo, Onur Koyun, Zhengjie Zhu, Abbas Alili, Metin N. Gurcan
papers.evidence: 通过分阶段编码器冻结和非平稳嵌入损失缓解码本坍塌
papers.tldr: 大码书向量量化模型常因编码器漂移导致码书坍塌：移动的潜在分布使稀疏更新的码向量滞后，形成反馈循环。NSVQ提出非平稳感知训练策略，通过密集非平稳嵌入损失、码书替换和分阶段冻结编码器，先帮助码书跟踪漂移，再固定编码器巩固码书，最后引入对抗精炼。在ImageNet-1k 128x128分辨率、65536码书设置下，rFID从2.39降至2.10，保持100%码书利用率。该方法有效抑制码书坍塌，提升图像重建与下游生成质量。
papers.abstract_en: Vector quantization is central to modern generative modeling pipelines, but large-codebook VQ models often suffer from codebook collapse. We identify encoder drift as a key driver of this failure: as the encoder moves the latent distribution, sparsely updated code vectors can lag behind, lose assignments, and increase quantization error, creating a feedback loop through the straight-through estimator. We propose NSVQ, a non-stationary-aware VQ training strategy that combines a dense non-stationary embedding loss, codebook replacement, and stage-wise encoder freezing. NSVQ first helps the codebook track encoder drift during early training, then freezes the encoder to consolidate the codebook under a fixed latent geometry, and finally reintroduces adversarial refinement. Experiments on ImageNet-1k show that NSVQ improves reconstruction quality while maintaining full codebook utilization. On ImageNet-1k at 128$\times$128 with 65,536 codes, NSVQ reduces rFID from 2.39 to 2.10 compared with SimVQ, while both methods maintain 100\% utilization. Additional latent diffusion experiments show that NSVQ also improves downstream ImageNet generation FID.

papers.title_en: EvoGS: Constructing Continuous-Layered Gaussian Splatting with Evolution Tree for Scalable 3D Streaming
papers.authors: Yuang Shi, Simone Gasparini, Géraldine Morin, Wei Tsang Ooi
papers.evidence: 连续层级表示用于可扩展3D流，直接关联可扩展神经视频编码
papers.tldr: 针对现有3D高斯泼溅流式传输中离散分层表示导致的误差累积、冗余严重和质量跳变问题，提出首个连续分层表示EvoGS。通过进化树结构实现小波启发的父子细化，使子节点可结构性地修正祖先误差，生成稀疏且可压缩的层间信号。实验表明，该表示将泼溅冗余从65%降至25%，传输载荷和GPU显存占用分别降低2.4倍和5.5倍，并实现了自适应流式传输所需的质量平滑过渡。
papers.abstract_en: Streaming 3D Gaussian Splatting requires highly scalable, progressive representations. Existing progressive methods rely on \textit{discrete layering}, accumulating separate splat sets for each level of detail. This structural independence between layers inherently leads to error accumulation, severe splat redundancy, and uncontrolled quality transitions. We propose EvoGS, the first \textit{continuous-layering} representation. Organized as an Evolution Tree, EvoGS generates finer details via an explicit, wavelet-inspired parent-child refinement. This empowers child nodes to structurally correct ancestral errors, yield inherently sparse and highly compressible inter-layer signals. Extensive experiments show EvoGS eliminates splat redundancy from over 65\% to under 25\%. Compared to state-of-the-art baselines, it reduces transmission payload and GPU VRAM footprint by up to 2.4$\times$ and 5.5$\times$, respectively, and achieves smooth quality transitions optimal for real-time adaptive streaming. Project page: https://yuang-ian.github.io/evogs/

papers.title_en: STAR-KV: Low-Rank KV Cache Compression via Soft Thresholding for Adaptive Rank Control
papers.authors: Priyansh Bhatnagar, Ashkan Moradifirouzabadi, Se-Hyun Yang, SeungJae Lee, Jungwook Choi, Mingu Kang
papers.evidence: KV缓存压缩中的低秩与量化训练技巧
papers.tldr: 现有低秩KV缓存压缩方法依赖固定秩选择，难以在激进压缩下保持精度。STAR-KV提出可微阈值机制实现注意力头级和块级自适应秩选择，结合混合分解与低秩感知量化。在多种LLM上达成75%压缩率，结合量化实现20倍整体压缩，注意力模块获得6.9倍加速。该方法首次实现细粒度自适应秩控制，有效兼顾压缩比与模型精度。
papers.abstract_en: Low-rank projection has emerged as a promising approach for compressing the KV cache by exploiting hidden-dimension redundancy. However, prior methods rely on fixed or heuristic rank selection and struggle to achieve aggressive compression with minimal accuracy degradation. We propose STAR-KV, an adaptive low-rank KV cache compression framework with fine-grained rank control. STAR-KV encompasses 1) a differentiable thresholding mechanism that enables optimal rank selection at both attention-head and block levels, 2) a hybrid decomposition strategy that applies different low-rank factorizations according to the sensitivity of key and value projections, and 3) a low-rank-aware mixed precision quantization that leverages data statistics for near lossless low-bit quantization. Evaluated across multiple LLMs and benchmarks, STAR-KV achieves up to 75% KV cache compression and up to 20x overall KV cache reduction when combined with quantization. Enabled by custom Triton-based GPU kernels, STAR-KV delivers up to 6.9x speedup for the attention module and 3.1x end-to-end generation throughput. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/PriyanshBhatnagar/STAR-KV.

papers.title_en: BiWM: Advancing Open-Source Interactive Video World Models with Bidirectional Autoregression
papers.authors: Shaohao Rui, Xiaofeng Mao, Zhanyu Zhang, Peijia Lin, Yansong Zhu, Yibo Zhang, Haibin Wan, Weijie Ma
papers.evidence: 双向视频扩散用于交互世界模型，与生成式视频压缩相关
papers.tldr: 现有因果自回归视频世界模型需多阶段训练且误差累积，而双向模型虽有优势但缺乏开源框架。为此提出BiWM，首个全栈双向自回归交互式视频世界模型框架，从预训练骨干通过微调和少量步DMD蒸馏实现可控交互，仅需两阶段训练。在多种大模型上实现真实相机控制与稳定长程rollout，附加GAN和forward-KL目标缓解模式坍塌。开源框架降低了资源需求，适用于高保真环境模拟。
papers.abstract_en: Transitioning bidirectional video diffusion models into an autoregressive paradigm improves the interactivity of video world models, but existing causal pipelines need many stages (control fine-tuning, autoregressive training, causal initialization, few-step distillation) and still trail bidirectional models in quality due to error accumulation. Recent world models such as Yume-1.5 and Matrix-Game-3.0 instead adopt a bidirectional autoregressive approach, gaining fidelity and stable long-horizon rollout from self-correcting error propagation, yet open-source frameworks (e.g., minWM) support only causal models. We present BiWM, the first full-stack framework for interactive video world models under the bidirectional autoregressive paradigm, jointly optimizing generation quality and inference speed. From a pretrained video backbone, BiWM injects camera control by fine-tuning, then runs a few-step Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD) stage that turns the backbone into an action/camera-controllable world model: just two training stages instead of four in minWM, converging in a few hundred steps on 8xH200 GPUs. A single recipe spans Wan2.1-1.3B, Wan2.2-5B, HunyuanVideo-1.5-8B, and LTX-2.3-22B, and also supports secondary fine-tuning of existing bidirectional models. BiWM enables real-world camera control where minWM loses controllability, integrates pluggable history compression (FramePack-style and PackForcing-style) for long rollouts, and offers an optional NVFP4 4-bit training/inference pipeline. To counter DMD's mode-seeking degradation, we add GAN and mass-covering forward-KL objectives that preserve scene dynamics. We open-source BiWM for resource-constrained research and high-fidelity environment simulation.
